rulings.Case Title: PARANEE RATREESAWAD ET AL VS. RANDOLPH D. CARUSO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 15, 2017, line 1. DEFENDANT SAED NIJEM's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion for summary judgment is DENIED. Motion for Summary Adjudication for causes of action for breach of contract, declaratory relief, quiet title and elder abuse is GRANTED. Moving party has shifted its burden. Opposing party failed to cite any authority or create triable issues of fact regarding moving defendant's liability for these causes of action. Motion for Summary Adjudication is DENIED as to causes of action for breach of warranty of quiet enjoyment and Negligent Infliction of Emotional Distress. Triable issues of fact exists regarding Plaintiffs' status as tenants; breaches of warranty of quiet enjoyment, and Plaintiffs' emotional distress. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: SCOTT DYKES VS. MERIDIAN MANAGEMENT GROUP, A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion And Notice Of Hearing On The Motion To Vacate The Default Entered Against Russell B. Flynn Investment Company, Llc And Russell B. Flynn
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 15, 2017, line 2. DEFENDANT RUSSELL B. FLYNN INVESTMENT COMPANY L.L.C., RUSSELL FLYNN's Motion To Vacate The Default Entered Against Russell B. Flynn Investment Company, LLC And Russell B. Flynn GRANTED. No opposition filed. Default is set aside. Defendants to file answers forthwith. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ANTREA INVESTMENTS AND TRADING LLC, VS. VANGUARD PROPERTIES INC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Strike Portions Of Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 15, 2017, line 3. DEFENDANTs VANGUARD PROPERTIES INC, FRANK NOLAN, CARLOS CABARCOS' Motion To Strike Portions Of Second Amended Complaint Granted in part and denied in part. Motion is DENIED as to the request for punitive damages. Allegations are sufficient. Motion is GRANTED with leave to amend as to the request for attorney's fees and disgorgement to be re-alleged if proper only if a viable cause of action for breach of contract is alleged. See ruling on demurrer. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ANTREA INVESTMENTS AND TRADING LLC, VS. VANGUARD PROPERTIES INC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 15, 2017, line 3. DEFENDANTs VANGUARD PROPERTIES INC, FRANK NOLAN, CARLOS CABARCOS' DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT Sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer is overruled as to the Complaint, second cause of action for constructive fraud and fifth cause of action for equitable indemnity. These causes of action are sufficiently alleged. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the breach of contract cause of action for Plaintiff to allege facts in support of an actionable breach of an express or implied covenant of the contract, specifying such covenant. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: POSEE CHUNG VS. RICK QURESHI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion; Motion To Quash Service Of Summons
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 15, 2017, line 8. DEFENDANT RICK QURESHI's Motion To Quash Service Of Summons DENIED. Service was accomplished by posting and mailing per the Court's order. The certified mailing tracking information indicates that it took the Post Office over 10 days to accomplish delivery (without any indication of failed attempts), which is twice the amount of time presumed by the Code of Civil Procedure for mail delivery. Under these unusual circumstances the Court finds that the service was accomplished on June 9, 2017 and Defendant has until June 26, 2017 to file a response. = (501/REQ)
